title: fraxinus americana var. epiptera
intro: Fraxinus americana var. epiptera, also known as the white ash, is a deciduous tree species native to eastern North America. The medicinal properties of this plant include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Fraxinus americana var. epiptera include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the bark or leaves in hot water or oil.
